subsystem: In-repo Agent Skills + macOS desktop eval/audit apps (the auditing & skill-packaging layer)
what_it_is: Seven Claude Code Agent Skills (SKILL.md = YAML frontmatter + markdown playbook) that wrap the repo's Python governance-enforcement scripts so an agent runs and interprets them correctly (real numbers + escalation posture) instead of skimming for a bare PASS/FAIL. Four of the audit skills are also packaged as double-click macOS .app bundles: a bash launcher runs a generate.py that shells the underlying script read-only and renders a live HTML dashboard to /tmp. One skill (pr-conflict-resolver) additionally ships an eval harness proving a +0.24 pass-rate lift versus no-skill.

current_state.note: Skills themselves are LIVE and complete: every script/data file they wrap was verified to exist on disk (audit_receipt_ledger_v1.py, agent_read_staleness_engine_v1.py, verify_agent_read_staleness_v1.sh, validate_parallel_automation_governance_v1.py, audit_automation_surface_v1.py, check.py, and all 5 referenced data/*.json). BUT: (1) only 1 of the 4 audit skills (pr-conflict-resolver) has an evals/ harness — the other three have none. (2) All 4 desktop apps HARDCODE REPO=/Users/sinakazemnezhad/Desktop/Noetfield-Systems/sina-governance-SSOT (the founder's Desktop clone, NOT this worktree) — verified that path exists, so they run only on the founder's Mac against a different checkout. (3) The PR-Conflict-Resolver app is the odd one out: it ships a frozen 85KB static report.html and does NOT regenerate, while the other three regenerate live each launch. (4) .app bundles are unsigned and hand-maintained — no build/packaging script exists in-tree; STYLE CSS + launcher bash are copy-pasted across bundles. Note: the four audit skills are in-repo files but are NOT in this session's active skill list, whereas the adjacent p0pgr-* trio is.

key_assets.purpose: Governance-aware git merge-conflict playbook: classifies each conflicted file (append-only receipt / structured registry / LOCKED canon / generated artifact / ordinary code / lane doc), does structural JSON registry merge via git show :2:/:3:, and escalates duplicate-owner / LOCKED conflicts instead of auto-picking.

Wraps verify_agent_read_staleness_v1.sh / agent_read_staleness_engine_v1.py; interprets blocker_count vs warn_count vs alive_surfaces, and flags the missing_read_surface false-blocker caveat for sibling-repo docs in sandboxed sessions.

Wraps audit_receipt_ledger_v1.py; interprets malformed_count / missing_timestamp_count / cluster_count (cross-motor same-second collisions) as post-hoc single-tree analog of the pr-conflict-resolver collision signal.

Wraps validate_parallel_automation_governance_v1.py + audit_automation_surface_v1.py; enforces L1 (one writer per task cell) and L4 (registry is routing truth): duplicate motor_id, unregistered workflow, missing task cell.
4-scenario eval suite (L1 registry violation, receipt-filename collision, LOCKED doc, generated sidecar) with per-scenario expectations[] — the schema template any other skill's evals would mirror.
Benchmark run output: with-skill 100% vs without-skill 76% pass rate (+0.24), 3 runs/config, with per-expectation evidence and analyst notes on which evals discriminate.

Reference generator pattern: subprocess.run([python3, scripts/<engine>, --json, --fail-on never], cwd=REPO) then renders a stat-row + blockers/warns/alive tables to /tmp/*.html. Read-only (no --write-receipt/--write-queue).
Same live-dashboard pattern for the receipt ledger audit (clusters/malformed/missing-timestamp cards); shares the identical STYLE CSS block with the other two generators.
Runs both governance validators and shows dual PASS/FAIL mini-badges + raw stdout/stderr; notes that audit_automation_surface_v1.py writes its own receipt each run by design.
Frozen 85KB static eval report (does NOT regenerate) — the only bundle that ships a snapshot instead of a live generate.py.
Bash launcher template (near-identical across all 4 apps): runs generate.py, then opens the /tmp HTML as a chromeless Chrome/Edge/Brave --app window with fallback to the OS default handler.
macOS bundle metadata (CFBundleIdentifier systems.noetfield.sg.*, CFBundleExecutable launcher, unsigned) — the manifest that would be stamped by any bundle-build script.

extension_points: Add a new skills/<name>/SKILL.md dir with the established frontmatter (name / description / compatibility) to register a new governance capability — the compatibility: block already declares the exact scripts+data a skill depends on.
generate.py contract: each wraps a script that emits --json and renders HTML to /tmp/<slug>-report.html; a new dashboard plugs onto any script that speaks --json.
The single REPO = Path('/Users/.../Desktop/.../sina-governance-SSOT') constant in all 3 live generate.py is the one pin point to parameterize (env var / git rev-parse / bundle-relative).
The identical STYLE CSS block (duplicated verbatim across 3 generate.py) and the near-identical launcher bash + Info.plist are factor-out points for a shared template.
evals/ schema (id / prompt / expected_output / expectations[] in evals.json; run_summary delta in benchmark.json) is a reusable harness — currently only pr-conflict-resolver uses it.

The four audit surfaces (staleness, receipt-ledger, registry/motor, pr-conflict) each already produce a machine JSON verdict, so they can be chained into one roll-up console or a single combined receipt.
compatibility: frontmatter lists each skill's required scripts/data — a lint pass can cross-check those declarations against files actually on disk.

buildable_ideas.idea: Build the 3 missing eval suites (staleness-gate-auditor, receipt-ledger-auditor, registry-motor-validator) mirroring skills/pr-conflict-resolver/evals/ — e.g. staleness: a stale-law pattern matching an ACTIVE surface must be reported as a named blocker not a bare FAIL; receipt-ledger: a cross-type same-second cluster must be surfaced not auto-picked; registry: a duplicate motor_id must be called an L1 violation. Gives all four skills the same +pass-rate evidence pr-conflict-resolver has.

Ship a unified 'Governance Console' HTML dashboard (or 5th .app) that runs all three live generate.py scripts plus a pr-conflict summary and shows one CLEAN/FINDINGS roll-up with the four sub-badges — the four generators already emit a common badge/stat-row vocabulary, so it's a compose, not a rewrite. All wrappers stay read-only (no --write-receipt), so it never mutates the tree.
buildable_ideas.builds_on: the three generate.py live-dashboard scripts + shared STYLE block + launcher pattern

buildable_ideas.idea: De-pin the apps: change the hardcoded REPO constant in all 3 generate.py to resolve the repo root from a NOETFIELD_SG_REPO env var, then git rev-parse --show-toplevel, then bundle-relative fallback — so the apps run on any checkout (incl. sandboxes/CI) instead of only the founder's Desktop clone. Small, surgical, unblocks every other app idea.
buildable_ideas.builds_on: REPO = Path('/Users/.../Desktop/.../sina-governance-SSOT') in the 3 generate.py + the launcher's DIR resolution

buildable_ideas.idea: Give PR-Conflict-Resolver-Report.app parity with the other three: replace its frozen 85KB static report.html with a generate.py that renders skills/pr-conflict-resolver/evals/benchmark.json live (pass-rate delta, per-eval expectations, analyst notes) so reopening the app reflects the latest benchmark run instead of a snapshot.
buildable_ideas.builds_on: evals/benchmark.json + the generate.py render pattern from the other bundles

buildable_ideas.idea: Add a build/packaging script (scripts or desktop-app/build.py) that stamps out a .app bundle from a shared launcher template + shared STYLE.css + a per-app config (name, bundle id, generator) — eliminating the copy-pasted CSS/launcher/Info.plist and making a new audit-app a one-line config addition. Codifies the currently hand-maintained bundle layout.
buildable_ideas.builds_on: the 3 identical STYLE blocks, 4 near-identical launcher scripts, and the *.app/Contents/Info.plist manifests

buildable_ideas.idea: Write a skill-manifest self-linter (skill + tiny script) that parses every skills/*/SKILL.md compatibility: block and asserts each referenced script/data path actually exists on disk — catching a skill that points at a moved/renamed script, which is the same 'authority pointing at something that no longer resolves' failure mode staleness-gate-auditor exists to catch, but applied to the skills layer itself. I verified the wrapped files exist today; this keeps it true.
buildable_ideas.builds_on: the compatibility: frontmatter in all 7 SKILL.md files + the existence-check pattern (all wrapped scripts/data confirmed present)
